turns.user_message: My chest feels like there is a heavy weight sitting right on top of my ribs, and it has been lingering for weeks now. I catch myself holding my breath without even realizing it until my shoulders start to ache. It is just this constant tension that makes it hard to take a proper, deep breath. I keep trying to ignore it, but it just sits there in the background of everything I do. It is exhausting to carry around, even when nothing particularly bad is happening.

That heavy feeling has completely taken over my body now, turning into a relentless buzzing under my skin that never really switches off. My heart starts racing over the smallest things, and my hands get so shaky I have to clench them just to keep still. I notice myself pacing around my room for hours, completely unable to settle down or find any kind of comfort. Every muscle feels wired and tight, like I am bracing for something that never actually arrives. It is incredibly draining to feel this wound up all the time without any clear reason.

The pressure inside my chest has become so suffocating that I can barely catch my breath, and my whole body feels like it is vibrating out of control. My stomach twists into tight knots every single moment, making it impossible to sit still or even think straight. I feel completely trapped inside my own skin, constantly bracing for a crash that keeps building and building without ever breaking. It is like my mind is stuck on maximum alert, flooding me with this raw, frantic energy that I cannot shake. I just want the physical storm to finally quiet down so I can feel normal again.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This has been going on for a while.
